perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company; or (ii) the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, "Termination for Cause" shall mean termination of this Agreement by the Company as the result of: (i) any act of fraud, dishonesty or neglect of services by CONSULTANT in connection with the services to be provided under this Agreement or against any Company customer, vendor or affiliated company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that defines "Termination for Cause" to include 'the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT', we create an omission that creates an in-text contradiction. Section 9.7(b)(i) refers to 'Any breach by Consultant of any representation, warranty or covenant contained in or made pursuant to this Agreement'. With the removal, it becomes unclear if a breach of contract can constitute a termination for cause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Indemnity by Consultant. Consultant hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Company and each person and affiliate associated with the Company against any and all losses, claims, damages, liabilities and expenses (including reasonable costs of investigation and legal counsel fees) to the extent not funded by applicable insurance, in addition to any liability the Consultant may otherwise have, arising out of, related to or based upon: (i) Any breach by Consultant of any representation, warranty or covenant contained in or made pursuant to this Agreement; or (ii) Any violation of law, rule or regulation by Consultant or Consultant's agents, employees, representatives or affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'the breach or prospective breach of any provision of this Agreement by CONSULTANT' from the definition of 'Termination for Cause.' This omission directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states 'Any breach by Consultant of any representation, warranty or covenant contained in or made pursuant to this Agreement.' The changed text now creates uncertainty as to whether a breach of contract constitutes grounds for termination for cause, creating a direct and strong contradiction and undermining the enforceability of the indemnity clause.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Customer for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Customer to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company. As used in this Section 7.2, "Customer" means any person or entity to which the Company provided products or services, including any tenants of the Company's properties, (or was invested in real estate owned by the Company), and with which CONSULTANT had contact on behalf of the Company, within the last twelve (12) months of its consultancy with the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Customer for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Customer to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'Customer' in Section 7.2, the meaning of the clause becomes ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the contract specifies what is restricted activity for the consultant, without specifying who those restriction apply to. It creates uncertainty in enforcement because the term 'Customer' is not defined, and could be interpreted very broadly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Customer for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Customer to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the definition of 'Customer' from Section 7.2 creates a direct conflict with the contradicted section, because the definition of who the consultant cannot solicit, entice, or induce is no longer specified. This ambiguity renders the non-solicitation clause difficult to enforce and creates uncertainty about the scope of the consultant's obligations, as the term 'Customer' is now undefined. This is an omission in text contradiction because the original text contained a definition that was then removed, creating contradiction. The contradicted text references a section in the changed text, which is not the same as the original.

perturbation.original_text: 8.3 Change of Control. In the event of a 'Change of Control' of Company, then if CONSULTANT's services are terminated without cause at any time within a twelve (12) month period following such Change of Control, CONSULTANT shall receive a termination fee equal to twelve (12) months compensation hereunder at the then current monthly rate, including any stock compensation. A Change of Control shall be any purchase of equity in the Company by a party that results in the party acquiring (i) voting control of the Company or (ii) the authority to replace or appoint, a majority of the Board, or the CEO or COO or CFO of the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.3 Change of Control. In the event of a 'Change of Control' of Company, then if CONSULTANT's services are terminated without cause at any time within a twelve (12) month period following such Change of Control, CONSULTANT shall receive a termination fee equal to twelve (12) months compensation hereunder at the then current monthly rate, including any stock compensation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the definition of 'Change of Control' in Section 8.3, it leads to ambiguity on when the consultant is eligible for a termination fee. This is an in-text contradiction because, while the section specifies a consequence related to a 'Change of Control,' it no longer defines what that term means. Therefore, the application of the clause is uncertain, especially in determining when the additional compensation is due.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.3 Change of Control. In the event of a 'Change of Control' of Company, then if CONSULTANT's services are terminated without cause at any time within a twelve (12) month period following such Change of Control, CONSULTANT shall receive a termination fee equal to twelve (12) months compensation hereunder at the then current monthly rate, including any stock compensation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the definition of 'Change of Control' from Section 8.3. The contradicted text relies on this definition to determine when the consultant is eligible for a termination fee. Without the definition, the contradicted text becomes ambiguous and unenforceable, creating a direct in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.
